title: UK Consumer Duty Fair Value Assessment
content:
MERIDIAN WEALTH PARTNERS - UK CONSUMER DUTY FAIR VALUE ASSESSMENT

This assessment has been prepared in accordance with FCA PS22/9 Consumer Duty requirements and PRIN 2A obligations to ensure our investment products and services deliver fair value to retail clients.

PRODUCT SCOPE AND METHODOLOGY

Our fair value assessment covers discretionary portfolio management services, structured products, and collective investment schemes offered to UK retail clients with portfolio values between £150,000 and £5,000,000. We have applied a comprehensive framework evaluating price, quality, and total costs against comparable market alternatives.

For discretionary management services, our annual management charges range from 0.75% to 1.25% depending on portfolio complexity and size. Initial charges are capped at 2.5% for structured products. We benchmark these fees against 15 peer wealth managers serving similar client segments, finding our charges consistently within the second quartile.

PERFORMANCE AND OUTCOMES ANALYSIS

Over the 12-month assessment period ending March 2023, our balanced growth portfolios delivered net returns of 4.2% compared to the IA Mixed Investment 40-85% Shares sector average of 3.8%. Risk-adjusted returns using Sharpe ratios showed our strategies outperforming benchmarks by 0.15 on average.

Transaction costs across our platform averaged 0.18% annually, with custody charges of 0.12% representing competitive positioning versus industry standards. Third-party fund charges within model portfolios averaged 0.68%, demonstrating effective due diligence in fund selection.

CLIENT VULNERABILITY CONSIDERATIONS

Under Consumer Duty requirements, we have enhanced identification processes for vulnerable clients, particularly those experiencing major life transitions, cognitive decline, or financial distress. Approximately 12% of our client base has been flagged for additional support measures, with specialized communication protocols and extended cooling-off periods implemented.

REMEDIATION ACTIONS IDENTIFIED

Our assessment identified three areas requiring attention: (1) Communication materials require simplification to meet plain English standards for clients with lower financial literacy; (2) Exit charges on certain structured products exceeding 3% after year one need review; (3) Investment research distribution timing showed 15% of clients received reports more than five business days after others, creating potential fairness concerns.

Governance oversight has been strengthened with monthly Consumer Duty committee meetings chaired by our Chief Risk Officer. Client outcome metrics are now embedded in senior management reporting, with specific KPIs including complaint resolution times (target: 95% within 8 weeks), product switching frequency analysis, and systematic bias monitoring in advice recommendations.

CONCLUSIONS AND ONGOING MONITORING

Based on quantitative analysis and qualitative assessment, we conclude our core investment offerings provide fair value to target client segments. However, ongoing monitoring frameworks have been enhanced to ensure continuous compliance with evolving Consumer Duty expectations.

Quarterly reviews will assess fee competitiveness, performance attribution, and client feedback mechanisms. Any material changes to product terms, charging structures, or target market definitions will trigger immediate fair value reassessment.

This assessment covers the period from April 2022 to March 2023 and should be read alongside our annual Consumer Duty outcomes report submitted to the FCA. All advisors must familiarize themselves with these findings before engaging in client reviews or new business discussions.
